title: aglitheis tricoccum
intro: Aglitheis tricoccum, also known as the Eastern Mayapple, is a perennial plant native to the eastern regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as to support immune function and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of Aglitheis tricoccum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made from the plant's ripe fruit, leaves, and roots.
